meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Stop Slow Dev Speed
content.seo_description: Overcome slow development speed with expert project management. An intermediate guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Slow development speed stems from poor architectural planning and scope creep. By integrating structured project management with AI-driven tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate boilerplate, enforce modularity, and maintain focus, effectively cutting your development cycle time in half while building a production-grade portfolio project.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard of half-finished ideas. You start with ambition, but within days, the complexity of your own architecture creates a friction that grinds your progress to a halt. The code feels brittle, the features feel disconnected, and the dream of a finished SaaS app begins to fade into the background of your coursework.

But this isn't a failure of your ability; it is a failure of your system. Professional engineering isn't just about writing code—it is about managing the cognitive load of the build. By adopting rigid project management frameworks and leveraging AI-augmented workflows, you can reclaim your velocity and transform from a student coder into a high-output AI builder.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Stop trying to build everything. Use Antigravity to generate a strict, feature-gated roadmap that prevents scope creep before you write a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lean MVP scope for a [Project Name]. Define only the core user journey, list 3 essential features, and exclude all nice-to-have UI enhancements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project roadmap JSON based on this scope: [Paste Antigravity Output]'

steps.title: Architectural scaffolding
steps.description: Establish a robust directory structure. A clean foundation prevents the 'spaghetti code' that slows down later development stages.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable folder structure for a Next.js 14 app, including separate directories for services, hooks, and UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the file structure based on the architectural plan provided.'

steps.title: Automate Type Definitions
steps.description: Use AI to enforce strict typing across your data models to catch bugs before they manifest, saving hours of debugging time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate TypeScript interfaces for a database schema involving Users, Projects, and Tasks with relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create models.ts based on the generated interfaces.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build in small, modular chunks. Never tackle a feature that takes more than 4 hours without breaking it down further.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the 'User Authentication' feature into 4 sub-tasks with clear acceptance criteria.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the first sub-task: [Insert Sub-Task Description]'

steps.title: Automated Code Review
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scan your progress for anti-patterns and performance bottlenecks before committing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current codebase for performance issues and suggest 3 optimizations for faster load times.

pro_tips: Always commit after every single sub-task; it provides a psychological 'win' and a safe rollback point.
Use AI to write your documentation as you build, not after; it forces you to clarify your logic.
Never accept the first output from an AI; ask for 'three alternative approaches' to learn better patterns.
Limit your 'work in progress' to one feature at a time to keep your context window clean.
Build a 'trash' folder for experimental code so your main branch stays pristine and deployable.
content.next_steps: After completing your first MVP, deploy it to Vercel and write a case study on the project management challenges you overcame. This is what recruiters actually look for in a developer portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop the cycle of unfinished projects and start shipping real, high-impact software. The only difference between a student and a senior engineer is the discipline of their workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
